title: pinguicula mariae
intro:
Pinguicula mariae is a species of carnivorous plant belonging to the genus Pinguicula, commonly known as butterworts, characterized by their ability to capture and digest insects to supplement their nutrient intake.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various ailments. The medicinal properties of Pinguicula mariae are attributed to its high content of alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. 

Herbal preparations of Pinguicula mariae, often in the form of infusions or tinctures, have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
